title: Acronia paquishae
taxonomy: Acronia paquishae is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It is a genus of flowering plants in the Rubiaceae family. The genus Acronia is part of the tribe Spermacoceae. Acronia paquishae is also classified as a type of flowering plant that belongs to the order Gentianales. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Acronia paquishae, also known as Paquish's acronia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Acronia paquishae include iridoid glycosides, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Acronia paquishae include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat coughs, colds, and sore throats.
